checks.name: format_discipline
checks.detail: frontmatter_present: PASS -- reading.md line 1 is the H1 '# Errors & Exceptions'; no leading YAML '---' block. section_template: PASS -- exactly the six required '##' headings appear in order: Overview (L3), Key Concepts (L7), Worked Example (L132), In Practice (L179), Key Takeaways (L188), References (L196); none added/renamed/dropped. The inline ```mermaid fenced block (L13-53) sits inside Key Concepts and is the topic's expected exception-hierarchy diagram, not a template violation. meta_sidecar: PASS -- reading.meta.json exists beside reading.md, parses as valid JSON, and carries all four required fields: word_count=1960, estimated_read_minutes=10, citations_used=[1,2,3], sections=[Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References].

checks.name: length
checks.detail: Sidecar word_count=1960; independent prose+code count lands in the ~1900-2000 band. Deep profile target 1800-2200 words: within range (reported ~1960 as expected). Reading-aloud minutes words/230 ~= 8.5 min. Sidecar cross-check: estimated_read_minutes=10 at reading_wpm=200 (1960/200 ~= 9.8, rounded 10) is internally consistent.

checks.name: citations
checks.detail: Primary resource [1] (docs.python.org tutorial/errors) cited repeatedly: L9, L56, L58, L68, L90, L92, L114, L123, L181, L182, L183. Supporting [2] (docs.python.org library/exceptions) cited: L9(implied via [3]), L11, L56, L77, L92, L101, L105, L184. Supporting [3] (realpython python-exceptions) cited: L9, L183. Bibliography (L198-200) lists [1][2][3], matching the inline markers and the sidecar citations_used=[1,2,3]. No orphan markers, no orphan bibliography entries, no fabricated markers.

checks.name: no_forward_references
checks.detail: Exception mechanics (try/except/else/finally, hierarchy, raise, custom exceptions) are in-scope for 7.2 and taught here. The 7.3 Robust File Reader case study is forthcoming-only: L177 'the skeleton of the robust file reader you'll build in the forthcoming 7.3 case study' -- named as upcoming, not taught. No week 8+ material; the L5 mention of 'A3 -- Python Foundations Diagnostic (due W8)' is an assessment pointer, not teaching of week-8 content. Backward references (7.1 file handling, 6.1-6.3 inheritance, 5.2 dict.get) are legitimate prerequisites.

checks.name: faithfulness
checks.detail: Sampled 11 assertions, all trace to corpus: (1) syntax error caught before running, points with ^ arrow; exception is a runtime event (L9 -> corpus L41-45); (2) exceptions are objects/classes in an inheritance tree, BaseException->Exception (L11 -> L49); (3) catching a parent catches children, LookupError catches IndexError+KeyError (L56 -> L68,L149); (4) try/except int(input()) ValueError example (L60-66 -> L74-82); (5) capture with 'as err' (L70-75 -> L86-91); (6) open() missing file raises FileNotFoundError, with+try compose (L77-88 -> L95-106); (7) else runs only on success, finally always runs (L90 -> L112-113); (8) except checked top-to-bottom, first match wins, specific-before-parent (L92 -> L132,L149); (9) grouped except (IndexError, ValueError) tuple (L94-99 -> L153-158); (10) common builtins incl. IOError alias for OSError, ValueError vs TypeError pairing (L101-112 -> L160-173); (11) raise + custom exception subclassing Exception (L114-130 -> L177-202). Worked example read_count and expected output match corpus L211-256 verbatim. Zero unsupported assertions (threshold: fail if > 2).

checks.name: tone_fit
checks.detail: Beginner, plainspoken register. Average sentence length under 25 words. Concrete analogies present: read try/except/else/finally 'as a sentence' (L90); 'right type, wrong value' vs 'wrong type entirely' pairing (L112). Runnable Python 3 in every example (try/except ValueError, FileNotFoundError guard, grouped except tuple, raise, custom exception, and a complete read_count worked example) with correct expected output block (L165-175). No hedging phrases ('it is generally considered' / 'many experts believe' / 'various studies have shown') -- 0 occurrences.

verdict: PASS -- all six checks pass. Format discipline: no frontmatter, the fixed six sections in order, expected inline mermaid diagram inside Key Concepts, and a present/valid sidecar (word_count=1960, estimated_read_minutes=10, citations_used=[1,2,3], sections). Length ~1960 words within the deep 1800-2200 budget. Primary [1] and supporting [2]/[3] all cited with a matching bibliography and no fabricated markers. Exception mechanics are in-scope and taught; the 7.3 robust-reader case study is forthcoming-only and no week 8+ content is taught. 11 sampled assertions all trace to the corpus (0 unsupported). Beginner tone with analogies and runnable Python 3 throughout.
